Significant depsipeptide formation upon cleavage during PL synthesis, which can't be managed with neutral-basic conditions due to MalDapPh1: Manage through purification and accept yield loss\nPh2 & beyond: TFA-amide route, if successful, should alleviate the risk through enabling use of neutral-basic conditionsSignificant depsipeptide formation upon cleavage during PL synthesis, which can’t be managed with neutral-basic conditions due to MalDapPh1: Manage through purification and accept yield loss\nPh2 & beyond: TFA-amide route, if successful, should alleviate the risk through enabling use of neutral-basic conditions[DISCREPANCY] DISCREPANCY
Isomer level in PL GMP batch higher than GLP-tox batch (translates to DS mechanistically), which presents a clinical hold riskUse same approach as used for QM-GG: build the case that isomer is a variant and has no impact on safety, efficacy, and PK.Isomer level in PL GMP batch higher than GLP-tox batch (translates to DS mechanistically), which presents a clinical hold riskUse same approach as used for QM-GG: build the case that isomer is a variant and has no impact on safety, efficacy, and PK.[VERIFIED]
An impurity level in PL GMP batch is slightly higher than GLP-tox batch (not clear whether it's conjugatable), which presents a clinical hold riskWork with Tox to justify. If need be, identify impurity to confirm whether conjugatableAn impurity level in PL GMP batch is slightly higher than GLP-tox batch (not clear whether it’s conjugatable), which presents a clinical hold riskWork with Tox to justify. If need be, identify impurity to confirm whether conjugatable[DISCREPANCY] DISCREPANCY
Fc reference standard reevaluation results did not meet acceptance criterion for aggregate. Ref Std will be renewed based on scientific rationale in Mar'26 to support all spec tests, but cannot currently be used to execute FcRn binding assay (characterization test)1) Conduct lab experiments to determine if ref std is appropriate for use as a quantitative standard for FcRn binding assay - activity completed.\n\n2) Investigate root cause of ref std aggregate growth. Once cause is identified, consider manufacture of a new Fc reference standard.[DISCREPANCY] DISCREPANCY

Timeline makes establishing best practice controls to mitigate challenging.Kicker charge procedures developed that will drive reaction to completion in event of stalling. RSM lot for PV will be the same as for RS.[DISCREPANCY] DISCREPANCY
Not ARS friendly submissionWill not meet all ARS goals because there will not be 12 mon. of DP stability data from the commercial site to support the requirements of some Wave 1 countries at the time of BLA submission. This lack of real time stability data from the launch site may result in < 24 months of shelf-life at launch if the comparability justification is not accepted. (May need to accept shortened shelf life and some obsolescence until commercial site shelf life is obtained).DL-late shift in PSAR RSM route may result in new impurities with minimal time for inclusion in impurity control strategy to PVPSAR RSM and proposed DL unspecified impurity specification match so any impurity carried through would pass proposed specification. PV PSAR route will be used in last RS batch.[DISCREPANCY] DISCREPANCY
WuXi XDC has not been audited for a PAI yetWuXi XDC PLI audit has gotten push out from 1H26 to 1Q27. The Chinese NMPA audit will be in September 2026.mAb- The mAb charge variants specification is not high risk at WuXi, long-term this may be a concern over the product lifecycle given future tech transfersMitigation:Widen the charge variants spec at time of BLA submission. Explore possibility for a new charge variants method (including carboxypeptidase digest) which may allow a reset of specifications, either at submission or post-approval[DISCREPANCY] DISCREPANCY
Questions from EU and Switzerland for JZXB indicated that they may not accept the 36-month shelf-life which used extrapolated stability data. Our representative DP lot has 30 months of real time stability data which this is the dating currently on the EU and Switzerland clinical trial label. In the event that a lesser shelf-life is required （less than 30 months, new EU and Switzerland JZXB supplies will need to be created with the new shelf-life on the labels. It is estimated that this could be up to a 4 month in supplying the EU and Switzerland countries if risk is realized.Update: EU accepted this approach as outline below on 11May2026. The Ph1 DP batch 20230702 dating to support the Ph3 (DP2) shelf-life in JZXB is deemed representative. Currently, the team has 30 months of shelf life on our phase I DP batch, and our Ph3 (DP2) clinical supply material is labeled with 30 months of dating on the EU packaged product. Although the EU Health Authorities are not aware that we have 30 months of dating, their question suggests labeling the product with 24 months of shelf-life. The team plans to share the 30 months of real time stability data with the Health Authorities on the representative DP1 batch in response to the question, and request for a 30-month shelf life, which is aligned with our current label. The batch is representative because there are no changes to drug product strength and formulation. Drug substance and drug product formulations are identical and drug substance analytical comparability has been achieved. Analytical comparability of the drug substance used in both DP1 and DP2 has been demonstrated. Both long term and accelerated DP2 stability data and additional stress stability data indicate a stable DP2 drug product.mAb- Release of mAb material commercially manufactured in China doesn’t comply with ChP- For example, Mycoplasma requires 2 tests as ChP method not harmonizedMitigation: \nShort-term: Test mycoplasma for PV batches in accordance with both USP/Ph. Eur and ChP\nLong-term: Request Bioreliance to validate a single harmonized method[DISCREPANCY] DISCREPANCY

Although no impact seen to date in GMP batches, potential long-term variability or future facility tech transfer concernMitigation:1) Complete additional supplemental characterization studies to develop further understanding and further mitigate risk. API and DP have multiple stability risks: Hydrolysis, nitrosamine (NA), and disproportionation (freebase formation). (med risk, high impact)\nNitrosamine specific: NA is present at low ppm levels in DS and DP. (Low risk/low impact for advanced cancer; med risk/high impact for non advanced cancer)•Improved synthetic route to minimize starting levels of degradants and nitrosamine (NA). \n •Utilize protective packaging (desiccated bottles for DP) and define critical water activity \n •Continue cold chain storage of DS and DP. \n •Qualify degradants NA in tox and/or verify as human metabolites\n •Implement solid form and NA characterization testing for DS and DP\n •Extend in-use studies from 16 to 32 days. DASHBOARD SECTION 4 — Risk Register: Embedded "Portfolio Snapshot" Analysis Text
STATIC_ANALYSIS — hardcoded summary text rendered at the top of the Risk Register lens

What this is: the Risk Register lens displays a pre-generated, hardcoded paragraph of portfolio statistics (variable STATIC_ANALYSIS in the dashboard's source), dated 2026-06-09 in its own text. The live PROGRAMS[] / FLAT_RISKS[] arrays it should summarize are tagged with FEED_DATE = "2026-06-19" — a live-data feed date 10 days newer than the analysis text sitting right above it on the same page. Every quantitative claim below was checked directly against the live arrays in the same file.

Full text as rendered:
"Portfolio snapshot (2026-06-09): 131 programs (7 GC, 17 pGC, 107 CI). Red: Brenipatide, QM-GG Effort 1, Retatrutide. Yellow: AK-OTOF, Eloralintide, Lepodisiran, MAPT siRNA-BBB III ("814"), Monthly LAARA +7 more. Risks: 123 total (3 Red, 17 Yellow, 96 unassessed). Attention: 10 programs."

ClaimDashboard Text (verbatim)Live Data (PROGRAMS[] / FLAT_RISKS[], this file)Status
Total program count131 programs131 (live PROGRAMS[] count)[VERIFIED]
Priority tier breakdown7 GC, 17 pGC, 107 CI{'CI': 107, 'pGC': 17, 'GC': 7}[VERIFIED]
Red programs (named)Brenipatide, QM-GG Effort 1, Retatrutide (3 named, implies 3 total)Brenipatide, Insulin Efsitora Alfa, QM-GG Effort 1, Retatrutide (4 total)[DISCREPANCY] "Insulin Efsitora Alfa" is Red in live data but entirely absent from this list — undercounted by 1 (3 stated vs. 4 actual)
Yellow programs (named "+7 more")AK-OTOF, Eloralintide, Lepodisiran, MAPT siRNA-BBB III ("814"), Monthly LAARA +7 more (implies 12 total)AK-OTOF, Eloralintide, Lepodisiran, MAPT siRNA-BBB ("806"), MAPT siRNA-BBB Effort 4 ("807"), MAPT siRNA-BBB III ("814"), Monthly LAARA, Muvalaplin, Olomorasib, QM-GG Effort 2, QM-GGG Effort 1, QM-GGG Effort 2, Sofe-M (FRA ADC), Tirzepatide/Mounjaro/Zepbound (14 total)[DISCREPANCY] Actual Yellow count is 14, not 12 — undercounted by 2. Also unlisted even by name: "MAPT siRNA-BBB (\"806\")" and "MAPT siRNA-BBB Effort 4 (\"807\")"
Risk totals & assessment breakdown123 total (3 Red, 17 Yellow, 96 unassessed)135 total — {'': 102, 'Green': 9, 'TBD': 1, 'Yellow': 20, 'Red': 3}[DISCREPANCY] Actual total is 135, not 123 (off by 12). Red matches (3). Yellow is actually 20, not 17 (off by 3). "Unassessed" (blank) is 102, not 96 (off by 6). The stated breakdown doesn't even sum to its own stated total: 3+17+96=116 ≠ 123 — internally inconsistent before any comparison to live data.
Programs needing attention10 programsAK-OTOF, Brenipatide, Eloralintide, Insulin Efsitora Alfa, Lepodisiran, Muvalaplin, Olomorasib, QM-GG Effort 1, Retatrutide, Sofe-M (FRA ADC), Tirzepatide/Mounjaro/Zepbound (11 total)[DISCREPANCY] Actual is 11, not 10
Bottom line: of six checkable claims in this paragraph, two are correct (total program count, GC/pGC/CI split) and four are wrong — including an internal arithmetic error independent of the live data. A viewer reading only this summary text would materially undercount both Red and Yellow programs and the total risk count, and would not know Insulin Efsitora Alfa is Red at all.
DASHBOARD SECTION 5 — Ask the Portfolio (chat lens)
Suggested prompts (PILLS) and chat data grounding

This lens is a conversational interface backed by an LLM call, not a static data view — there is no fixed table of content to run a row-by-row verbatim audit against, since its responses are generated at query time. Two things on this tab are static and checkable: the seven suggested quick-prompts, and the underlying data the chat is grounded in (which is the same PROGRAMS[] / FLAT_RISKS[] / ALL_SCHED{} already fully audited above — no separate copy of the data exists for this tab).

Suggested PromptStatus
Portfolio overview[VERIFIED — portfolio-level query, no specific program to check]
Red programs[VERIFIED — portfolio-level query, no specific program to check]
Retatrutide risks[VERIFIED — references a real, existing program]
Lepodisiran milestones[VERIFIED — references a real, existing program]
Eloralintide workstreams[VERIFIED — references a real, existing program]
Kisunla team[VERIFIED — references a real, existing program]
Orforglipron status[VERIFIED — references a real, existing program]
All seven prompts reference programs or queries that exist in the live data. Since the chat's actual answers are generated live from the same PROGRAMS/FLAT_RISKS/ALL_SCHED data already audited in the other sections, any discrepancy already flagged there (e.g. Sofe-M's 23 missing risk records, the 13 programs absent from the Programs lens) would also silently limit or mislead what this chat tab can correctly answer about those same programs — this is not a new class of error, but an inherited one.

Discrepancy Log Summary

Every project × sub-tab combination that did not cleanly resolve to [VERIFIED], compiled across all sections above. 372 entries.
